reasoning: France are the clear market favorites at around +140-165 after reaching a third straight World Cup semifinal without conceding a goal in the knockout rounds, outscoring opponents 16-2 behind the tournament's most fearsome attacking trio in Mbappé, Dembélé and Olise. Their semifinal against Spain (the tournament's stingiest defense, having conceded just one goal in five matches) is effectively the true final, as the winner becomes the overwhelming favorite to lift the trophy. On the other side, England have surged up the board after a Bellingham-inspired extra-time win over Norway and now face a defending champion Argentina side that has looked shaky, relying heavily on Messi and surviving repeated late scares against weaker opponents.
